channel.title: True the Vote Newsletter
channel.description: Latest newsletters from True the Vote

item.title: When One Voice Echoes Across a Nation
item.description:
You Can Do It, Too

​

Hi Friend,

After last week's Presidential Address, one thing's become really clear: Americans are paying attention.

And the questions we've been getting? They haven't been about declassified documents or intelligence reports. They've been a lot simpler than that.

"This seems to be bigger than me. What can I do?"

We heard it over and over on TTVN Live yesterday, and Catherine's answer is one every volunteer needs to hear: Take the next step.

For sixteen years, TTV has worked to expose the vulnerabilities in our elections. But here's the thing—exposing the problem was never going to be enough on its own. Winning results occur when everyday people decide to become the solution.

In case you missed it...

We've spoken a little about our new documentary, Trapped: Detroit, for the last couple of weeks. Catherine released "The truth is out. TRAPPED is coming. Stand by." this past Saturday and I encourage you to check out the latest in that newsletter if you haven't yet, and watch yesterday's episode where we dive in a little more.

Back to the question, "What can I do?", this is exactly why a project like TRAPPED: Detroit is so important.

When most people hear "election fraud," they picture numbers, charts, and headlines. TRAPPED tells a different kind of story. It's about real people—folks whose identities were stolen, whose votes were cast without them ever knowing, whose communities suffered because someone else's voice got put in place of their own.

What you're about to see is the human cost of voter fraud.
- Catherine Engelbrecht | TRAPPED: Detroit

Citizens like Ramon Jackson have been fighting the good fight on the ground for years. He noticed ballot anomalies and went to work: he wrote letters, attended meetings, addressed officials face to face, and gathered his community to do the same. He has been ignored and dismissed, despite bringing the receipts.

Until now.

Ramon did not quit. The more he was ignored, the more he dug his heels in until his calls to expose the truth amplified.

One citizen. One mission. Unstoppable perseverance.

You can make a difference, too.

This movement was never built by a handful of people. It's been built by thousands of volunteers, each one willing to take another faithful step forward.

So whatever your next step looks like—checking your record through Scan. Check. Protect., signing up to be a poll worker, digging into legislation, filing an open records request, or just learning a little more than you knew yesterday—it counts. More than you may realize.

History gets changed by ordinary people who just refuse to stop showing up.

Thank you for being one of them.

Ever Onward!

"Do not despise these small beginnings, for the Lord rejoices to see the work begin." - Zechariah 4:10

item.title: The truth is out. TRAPPED is coming. Stand by.
item.description:
From Catherine

Hi Friend —

Thursday night, President Trump stood before the nation from the East Room of the White House and did something no president before him has had the courage to do: he told the American people the truth about the vulnerabilities in our elections. He announced the declassification of intelligence documents pointing to foreign interference, weaknesses in our voting systems, and serious questions about the accuracy of our voter rolls — and, most significantly, he put them online for every citizen to read. He didn't just speak the truth; he built a permanent, transparent channel to keep telling it. Because of that, things will never be the same.

For sixteen years, True the Vote has been sounding these same alarms — often alone, often at great cost. To watch the President of the United States stand up and say them from the White House was a milestone all those years had been building toward.

Since Thursday, our inbox has been full of one question, asked a hundred ways: is True the Vote's work part of this?

The documents detail the Chinese Communist Party's compromise of American voter data on a staggering scale — what the President described as potentially the largest compromise of election data in history — that is the exact threat our investigation into Konnech surfaced years ago, when almost no one wanted to hear it. Notably, that same phrase — the "largest compromise ... in history" — echoes language used by the Assistant District Attorney in Los Angeles County in the Konnech matter (documents linked below). But declassified documents are being released in tranches, not all at once. What's public today is a first installment, not the whole file. I expect our specific findings to be reflected as more is declassified in the days and weeks ahead — and when they are, you'll be among the first to know. You can read the China findings released so far for yourself at WhiteHouse.gov/election-integrity.

As far as the geospatial (cell phone) research that later informed 2000 Mules, that was not included as far as we're aware — but that wasn't a shocker. While the files may ultimately appear among the declassified docs, the FBI proved long ago that they are only interested in cell phone data when it serves their narrative (think J6 protestors being tracked on cell phones, with arrests just days after the event). Wouldn't be at all surprised if those files never surface at all. We'll see.

Our other work around that time — from the yeoman's effort on identifying illegalities in voter rolls, winning the federal lawsuit in Georgia; a series of other bizarre lawsuits involving us (I'll leave it at that for the moment) in which the FBI was tangentially involved, I believe against us; the Grand Jury convened in secret against Gregg Phillips and myself … my reports to the FBI of having been poisoned … prison … it was a lot.

A lot.

I don't know how much of that will ultimately be included. But whatever it is, it is. I bear the memories and the scars. And I'm thankful for all of it, because it has led us to this moment.

I was pensive going into Thursday. In this environment, a single sentence can be lifted out of context and turned into a lifetime of false narratives. But the President managed it masterfully. He set honest expectations, he stayed disciplined, and he opened a new and durable channel for disclosure that will serve this country for years to come. That is leadership, and it deserves our full-throated support.

So here is where I stand. I believe the 2020 election was, in fact, stolen from President Trump. I also understand that the stakes of that statement could not be higher, and that the full weight and force of Chaos will continue to be used to grind down truth tellers. They can surely make things difficult, but they cannot stop truth from finding a way. Therefore, True the Vote will keep doing what we've always done — follow the evidence, show our work, and stand our ground — as the truth comes into the light.

Thank you for standing with us. Keep the faith. Never quit. Ephesians 6 FTW

Ever onward —

PS — some of you may have caught a sneak peek of the trailer for our new documentary, TRAPPED (Detroit), making the rounds on Instagram. We're holding off on posting it here (or anywhere else) for the moment, and the reason is simple: the shadow banning has already begun. It's hard enough to reach your inbox without landing in Spam, and I won't risk this whole email getting buried by adding a link they're clearly watching for. So consider this your heads-up — it's coming soon. It is real. And I could not be prouder of it. If you can't wait, you'll find it on Pastor Lorenzo Sewell's Instagram at @lorenzosewell. Until then, stand by. We're fortifying our flanks for the battle ahead.

PPS — if you can help us with a donation, we will put it to immediate use.

Stand With True the Vote

We can't do this without you — and we wouldn't want to.

Thank you for your support!

In Case You Missed It...

POTUS Addresses the Nation

2020 all over again? Several networks cut away from the President's address — though they made sure to watch closely enough to publish their tired rebuttals the next morning. We all know why. The newly released intelligence assessments, spanning 2020 to 2026, confirm what we've said for years: the vulnerabilities in our elections were never hypothetical. This is only the first tranche, and we're eager to see what else comes to light. After all, sunlight is the best disinfectant.

View More

They Knew. And Argued Anyway.

Days after the 2020 election, we helped Georgia citizens challenge 300,000+ inaccuracies in the voter rolls ahead of the December runoffs. Fair Fight took us to court. We won. They appealed, and we’re still awaiting the ruling. But the record is clear: 1 in 8 voter registrations is inaccurate. Dirty rolls are a gateway to election fraud.

Read More

And Then...

The GA challenges weren’t the only thing in motion. As the Fair Fight case unfolded, informants came forward with reports of ballot trafficking. We invested in geospatial cell phone data to investigate patterns around drop boxes in AZ, GA, PA, MI, and WI, then shared our findings with the FBI and sought help from state and local law enforcement—to no avail. The findings were later featured in 2000 Mules.

Detractors say the work was disproven. Not true. Here's more of the story you may have missed.

Watch the Video

On to Konnech

Our investigation exposed an election software company with deep and troubling ties to China. Before any law enforcement raid, we had already uncovered and turned over millions of records tied to the company and its election-worker management systems — including who could access sensitive data and who held administrative control. For telling the truth, we were rewarded with a poisoning, a lawsuit, jail, a secret Grand Jury investigation, and an FBI investigation that led exactly nowhere. But— we were right.

View the Collection

Looking Back: Predicted or...Hidden in Plain Sight?

The Most Important Video of the 2020 Election

In the summer of 2020, we went on record to describe how we believed COVID would become the crisis vehicle to sow chaos in the November elections. Today, it's never been more clear that the attempted decline of our nation was orchestrated, including the "plandemic".

Watch

The Enemy

In August 2023, we put out a warning: Stand and fight, or lose America to the evil communists, Marxists, media, politicians, and the deep state.

They're still at it. And we still fight.

Watch

Lead from Where You Stand

You can check your own voter registration record right now. Visit truethevote.org/registration and take three minutes to:

  1. Confirm your current record is accurate — your name, address, and status.
  2. Check the rolls in states where you used to live — old registrations often linger for years after you've moved, sometimes without your knowledge.
  3. Tell us what you found. Good or bad, we want to hear from you — and if something looks off, we're here to help you fix it.

Tools for Your Toolkit

You weren't meant to do this alone. Find your community, your tools, your purpose.

Become a Member

Stay ahead of the legislation shaping your elections and know when it's time to act.

Join LegiTrack

Until Next Week

Remember: you don't need a title, a law degree, or anyone's permission to get involved. You need awareness, courage, and the resolve to stay in it when others walk away.

True the Vote will keep doing our part. With faith as our foundation and patriots like you standing in the gap, we'll keep pressing for transparency, accountability, and an America we leave stronger than we found it.

Ever onward.

​

Stand With True the Vote

We can't do this without you — and we wouldn't want to.

Thank you for your support!

Grounded in Truth

A weekly word from the TTV team. Want one every day? Join us in TTV Now.​

Father, from the very beginning, You have called us to walk by faith, not by sight. You have never once asked us to place our confidence in what we can see. So before we focus on everything that we can see in front of us, we choose to focus on You. You are the same God who parts seas, moves mountains, opens prison doors, and brings light into the darkest places. When there are moments when circumstances seem heavier than they really are, remind us that You are the God Who never changes.

And Father, when the enemy wants us to believe that doors are closing, that obstacles are too great, or that hope is fading, remind us that what we see is only a small part of what You are doing. We only see the chapter we are living in, but You already know the ending. And not only do You already know the ending, but You are the Author Who wrote the ending. What appears dark to us is never dark to You. You are already working behind the scenes, arranging what we cannot see, protecting us from what we do not know, and preparing what we have not yet imagined.

Thank You for always being present and for always working things for our good.

In Jesus’ Name Amen

~ Jenna Riggs

For more prayers and encouragement from Jenna, join our Devotionals Space in the TTVN Community!

item.title: You Didn't See This Coming...
item.description:
Scan. Check. Protect. v2 and You

​

Hi Friend,

What if the most important work starts a lot closer to home?

There has been a growing emphasis on citizen participation—not just at the polls or behind voter registration tables (though those remain incredibly important), but through knowledge.

An informed citizen is an empowered citizen. That means understanding:

  • How legislation moves and where your voice can influence the process.
  • The power you have as a citizen and voter beyond Election Day.
  • The tools available to protect your own vote and help others protect theirs.
  • How election systems actually work, so you can separate fact from speculation.
  • Where to find trustworthy information instead of relying on headlines or social media.
  • How one small action, taken by millions of citizens, can create meaningful change.

Yesterday on TTVN Live, we gave the first public look at Scan. Check. Protect. v2—the next generation of one of True the Vote's best-known initiatives. Honestly, we think it's going to change how volunteers get involved.

To recap: most people don't think about their voter registration until Election Day. But every year, millions of us move, change names, or update addresses—and our records don't always keep up. If you're not checking your registration, you simply don't know what's on it.

On the live show, Vico stated it perfectly: you review your bank statement for anything odd. Your vote deserves that same attention.

True the Vote has analyzed voter rolls on a massive scale for years, and that work continues to evolve to put these tools in your hands. We've learned that real change happens when individual citizens take ownership of their own records.

Check out results from v1 👇👇👇

True the Vote
@TrueTheVote
Scan. Check. Protect!🚨 Thanks to our new initiative to clean the voter rolls, we’ve already talked to tons of people out here in Las Vegas, Nevada who are on voter rolls in States where they no longer live! Please do your part! Check to see if you too are still on voter rolls
10:39 PM • Jan 27, 2024
353
Retweets
586
Likes
Read 18 replies
​

What to expect inside SCP v2 in addition to verifying your record:

  • If your state lists it, review your voter history
  • See if other registrations show up at your address
  • Catch potential issues before Election Day
  • Get state-specific guidance if something looks off
  • Report what you find...without guessing or digging through a dozen .gov websites.

And here's what excites us most: this is the volunteer opportunity anyone can do. Picture volunteers everywhere helping friends, family, church groups, veterans, and neighbors spend a few minutes confirming their registrations. No training, no technical background—just citizens helping citizens. Small actions, multiplied across thousands of communities, add up to something big.

Working a voter registration table? Change the game. Bring Scan. Check. Protect. with you. Citizens can register or verify their voter registration instantly, eliminating paper forms, protecting sensitive information, and replacing bulky, costly materials.

Yesterday's TTVN Live includes a full walkthrough and live demo of v2—how it works, why we built it, and how you can start using it the moment it launches.

👉 Watch here: https://rumble.com/v7cq51m-ttv-now-live-new-segment-potus-and-the-path-forward.html​

We're putting the finishing touches on it now and we can't wait to hand it to you, because in your hands is where the change happens.

Ever Onward!

"Know well the condition of your flocks, and give attention to your herds." -Proverbs 27:23

​

item.title: When Government Picks Sides
item.description:
​

The Cost of Unequal Enforcement

​

Hi Friend,

At True the Vote, one of our core missions is education which often means taking a step back to understand the events that brought us here. History provides context, legislation creates the framework, and court decisions establish precedent. By understanding how yesterday shaped today, we can better appreciate how the decisions being made today will shape tomorrow.

Some background

In 2013, Congress held hearings into something most Americans never imagined possible: a federal agency admitting it had singled out organizations because of their names and beliefs.

The Internal Revenue Service acknowledged that when certain groups applied for tax-exempt status, it had flagged them based on what they were called and what they stood for—not on anything they had actually done:

Independent Sector is deeply troubled that applications for tax exempt status were singled out by IRS personnel for additional scrutiny based on the applying organizations’ names or the nature of their exempt activities. The IRS’s proper role in approving, and subsequently regulating, exempt organizations is not determining the worthiness of their cause, but their compliance with the law.

After years of harassment, unequal treatment, fines, and government investigations that extended beyond the scope of their tax-exempt applications, the organizations under scrutiny could have given up. The easier path would have been to throw up their hands, say, "Forget it," and walk away.

But they didn't.

Catherine was amongst the group of citizens who chose to do the hard thing: fight.

​

Regardless of political affiliation, one principle should unite Americans:

Government agencies should apply the law—not political preferences.

Here's what happened, how this applies today within LegiTrack, and which bills we're tracking this week.

In case you missed it:

For roughly 18 months beginning in 2010, employees in the IRS Determinations Unit flagged applications for tax-exempt status using keywords like "Tea Party," "Patriots," and "9/12"—later expanding to groups focused on government spending, debt, taxes, and the Constitution. What surfaced at the 2013 hearings was straightforward:

  • The IRS acknowledged that its screening had been inappropriate.
  • The Inspector General confirmed the criteria were improper—based on names and beliefs, not activities.
  • Some organizations waited nearly three years for a decision, and dozens faced improper requests for information like donor lists.
  • Congress spent months investigating how it happened and who knew.
  • And Americans across the spectrum asked one question: "Can government be trusted to treat everyone equally?"

So why revisit a hearing from more than a decade ago?

Because the question never changed. While the agency may be different, the issue isn't.

When oversight becomes precedent...

The IRS controversy didn't end when the hearings adjourned. What Congress exposed, the courts took up—years of litigation, rulings, and settlements that reinforced longstanding constitutional protections: the First Amendment rights to free speech and association, and the principle that government cannot selectively burden citizens for their political views.

Court decisions don't just settle a single dispute—they become part of the framework that guides future conduct. Agencies weigh those rulings when writing policy, legislators draft with those guardrails in mind, and citizens gain clearer standards for challenging overreach.

Oversight revealed the problem. The courts helped establish the principles that make similar conduct harder to justify. It's a reminder that legislation, oversight, and the judiciary each play a distinct but essential role in preserving constitutional government—today's bills become tomorrow's laws, and those laws may one day be tested in court, shaping the precedents that protect future generations.

The same principle applies to elections:

This is where LegiTrack comes in.

Election officials wield enormous authority over voter rolls, ballots, investigations, certification, and public records. Whether those powers are exercised fairly—or even appear to be exercised fairly—determines public confidence.

The IRS story wasn't ultimately about tax forms. It was about whether a government body could be trusted to treat citizens the same regardless of their views. Elections raise the same test, just in a different arena. When a voter is added or removed from a roll, when a ballot is handled and stored, when a records request is answered or delayed, the question underneath is identical: is the law being applied evenly?

Trust is created by transparent laws that apply equally to everyone.

Every week, legislatures introduce, amend, and debate bills that determine how elections are administered. Some move quickly. Others remain active for months before becoming law—or becoming the subject of litigation.

​LegiTrack follows them throughout that process because the final vote isn't always the most important part of the story. Some expand or limit oversight, increase or reduce public confidence, or raise new questions entirely.

Here's what's on our radar (and a few you may have missed):

1. Federal — H.R. 9577, the "COCOA-VIP Act" (introduced July 1, 2026) · Observer access. Amends the Help America Vote Act to affirm congressional election observers' access to federal primary elections.

Watch: How Congress defines oversight—and who is entitled to observe federal elections.

2. New Jersey — S 4523 (Introduced June 25, 2026 but sitting) · Audits and citizenship verification. Requires the Secretary of State and Motor Vehicle Commission to audit voter registrations going forward, and requires proof of citizenship to register at the MVC.

Watch: How the state balances election security with standards for verifying voter eligibility. Right now is a great time to reach out to your state reps to be heard.

3. Michigan — SB 1087 (Introduced) · Money in elections. Would revoke the ability of limited liability companies to spend money supporting or opposing candidates, parties, committees, or ballot initiatives; such expenditures would be invalid and subject to legal action, including possible dissolution, with carve-outs for existing obligations and certain media activities. Tie-barred with SB 1085 and SB 1086, so it takes effect only if those pass.

Watch: Where lawmakers draw the line on political spending and who may participate in elections financially.

4. Federal — S 4774, the "FAIR Elections Act of 2026" (introduced June 10, 2026 and also sitting) · Voter-roll integrity. Amends voter registration law to bar removing names from the rolls based on unverified databases, and prohibits false AI-generated election media.

Watch: How the bill defines "reliable" voter-roll maintenance while limiting the use of certain data sources.

5. New York — S 10563 (First Chamber) · Ballot handling. Requires a notice on all mail-return ballots explaining timely return methods, including information on drop boxes.

Watch: How election officials communicate ballot return requirements and the role of voter education in ballot administration. Keep an eye out for language edits and curveballs as it progresses forward.

History rarely repeats itself in exactly the same way, but it often asks the same questions.

Should government operate transparently? Should laws be applied equally? Should citizens be able to verify what government is doing?

Those aren't partisan questions. They're constitutional ones.

We'll keep watching the legislation that helps answer them.

Ever onward -

If you can, please consider donating to help us continue building tools like LegiTrack. https://truethevote.org/donate​

Help Us Stay in the Fight

Follow our new LegiTrack socials:

Truth Social: @LegiTrack | X: @Real_LegiTrack​
